data.title: Aptos Announces Tokenomics Update with Key Adjustments
data.description: On April 14, Aptos released an update to its tokenomics. According to BlockBeats, the main changes include a reduction in the annualized staking rewards rate from 5.19% to 2.6% and a tenfold increase in gas fees, although stablecoin transfer costs remain low at approximately $0.00014. The launch of Decibel DEX is expected to significantly boost on-chain transaction volume and gas fee burning, with an estimated annual burn of over 32 million APT. A protocol-level hard cap has been set for the total supply at 2.1 billion APT. Additionally, the Aptos Foundation will permanently lock and stake 210 million APT. Future incentives will be triggered by milestones, and there is exploration of a programmatic buyback plan.

data.title: SanDisk Stock Surges 11.83% as Company Joins Nasdaq-100
data.description: SanDisk's stock experienced a significant increase, closing at $952.50 on Monday, marking an 11.83% rise. According to NS3.AI, this surge follows the company's announcement that it will join the Nasdaq-100 on April 20, replacing Atlassian. This development propelled the stock to a new 52-week high of $953.41, boosting its market capitalization to approximately $140.59 billion. The rally is further supported by AI-driven demand for NAND and the upcoming earnings release on April 30.

data.title: BITHUMB will launch ZAMA Korean Won exchange rate.
data.description: According to the official announcement, BITHUMB will launch the ZAMA Korean Won pair.

data.title: KKR Advises Credit Managers on Portfolio Diversification Amid Market Volatility
data.description: Credit managers are being advised to prioritize portfolio diversification in response to heightened market volatility, according to KKR. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing conflict in Iran as a contributing factor to the current financial instability. KKR emphasizes the importance of spreading investments across various sectors to mitigate risks associated with unpredictable market conditions. The firm suggests that a diversified portfolio can help credit managers navigate the challenges posed by geopolitical tensions and economic uncertainties. This approach is seen as a strategic measure to safeguard investments and ensure long-term financial stability.

data.title: Planet Midday News
data.description: 1. X Product Manager: The crypto market has experienced a difficult year, and new projects may be launched to improve the situation; 2. Hyperliquid rejected a VC's $1 billion valuation investment terms in early 2024; 3. US banks question the White House report on stablecoin yields; 4. Trump used the ceasefire agreement to block the Strait of Hormuz, causing Iran daily economic losses of approximately $435 million; 5. Circle founder confirms Arc Network will launch its own token, with more details to be disclosed later; 6. Associated Press: The US and Iran may hold a new round of talks on Thursday; 7. Binance will delist UTK and support its exchange for XMN; 8. A whale holding over $100 million in assets increased its short positions in BTC and ETH, incurring cumulative losses of over $66.19 million; 9. Binance will add watch tags to seven cryptocurrencies including FARM and remove the XAUT seed tag.

data.title: Hyperliquid founder: He started with $10,000 in savings and achieved financial freedom at age 27.
data.description: Hyperliquid founder Jeff Yan and his team recently gave a feature interview to Colossos magazine. In the interview, Jeff discussed his career prior to founding Hyperliquid in 2022. Before founding Hyperliquid, Jeff almost single-handedly ran one of the largest anonymous trading teams in the cryptocurrency industry at the time—Chameleon Trading, a nickname he used while playing games in high school. Starting with $10,000 in savings, Jeff achieved thousands of percent annual growth in two and a half years, becoming financially independent at the age of 27. By 2022, the Chameleon Trading team had expanded to six people. However, Jeff felt that the crypto industry had done little substantial progress beyond Bitcoin and Ethereum, and the two largest exchanges (Binance and Coinbase) remained centralized, burying Satoshi Nakamoto's original vision for Bitcoin. Therefore, amidst this industry unease and the impact of the FTX collapse, Jeff terminated Chameleon Trading and began building the decentralized exchange Hyperliquid. For more details, please refer to Jeff Yan's "Hyper Life".

data.title: Hyperliquid Faces Challenges with API Servers Amid Bitcoin Surge
data.description:
Hyperliquid founder Jeff Yan and his team were recently featured in an exclusive interview with Colossos magazine. According to Odaily, during the interview, Jeff Yan was asked about the biggest challenges anticipated in 2025. He did not mention the attack on JLP by JellyJelly or any competitors, but instead highlighted issues with API servers.

Throughout the summer, as Bitcoin surpassed $100,000, Hyperliquid's monthly trading volume exceeded $400 billion. The servers connecting market makers to the blockchain began experiencing problems. With an increasing number of institutions joining, each sending massive volumes of orders, cancellations, and updates, the infrastructure struggled to keep pace. Orders that should have been executed instantly were delayed by up to three seconds. Although the blockchain itself remained operational and user funds were secure, a three-second delay is significant in a market where milliseconds matter.

Jeff Yan noted that during those weeks, he barely had any normal sleep, often going to bed at 1:30 a.m. and being woken up by 3 a.m. due to recurring system issues. Ultimately, the team rewrote the entire server system from the ground up, and the new system successfully withstood the extreme market conditions of the 1011 event.

For more details, refer to 'Jeff Yan's Hyper Life.'

data.title: US banks question White House report on stablecoin yields.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reports that the US banking industry has questioned the White House's report on stablecoin yields, arguing that its conclusions are based on flawed questions. The White House Council of Economic Advisers report states that banning stablecoin yields would have a negligible impact on bank lending, increasing loans by only about $2.1 billion. American Bankers Association Chief Economist Sayee Srinivasan and Vice President Yikai Wang stated that the core policy concern is whether allowing stablecoins to generate yields would lead to deposit outflows, particularly from community banks to large institutions, thereby increasing funding costs and reducing local lending. The American Bankers Association acknowledges that the financial incentive to pursue high-yield stablecoins would prompt households and businesses to move funds out of banks. Currently, the crypto and banking industries are negotiating the terms of a Senate bill, with a ban on stablecoin interest payments being a key point of contention.

data.title: XRP Gains 3% Amid Strong Trading Volume
data.description: XRP experienced a 3% increase, reaching $1.37 after climbing from $1.32 and surpassing the $1.35 mark on robust trading volume. According to NS3.AI, the cryptocurrency must break through the $1.42–$1.45 range to confirm a more extensive breakout.

data.title: The founder of Hyperliquid previously worked on a prediction market-like project called Deaux in 2018, but it failed due to a bear market.
data.description: Odaiy Planet Daily reports that Hyperliquid founder Jeff Yan and his team recently gave a feature interview to Colossos magazine. In the interview, Jeff revealed that after leaving quantitative trading giant Hudson River Trading in April 2018, he worked on Deaux, a prediction market-like project that would allow users to bet on any event with a result, such as weather, elections, or sporting events. Jeff experimented with an "off-chain matching, on-chain settlement" mechanism in the project (Jeff believes this was an idea he and co-founder Scott Wu pioneered). Funds would be stored in smart contracts controlled by code, but the user experience would remain fast and smooth, thus preserving the promise of cryptographic decentralization while avoiding its frictional costs. However, Deaux ultimately only had 100 users and was subsequently shut down due to a bear market. More details can be found in "Jeff Yan's Hyper Life".

data.title: In early 2024, Hyperliquid rejected a VC's investment offer with a valuation of $1 billion.
data.description: Odaiy Planet Daily reports that Hyperliquid founder Jeff Yan and his team recently gave a feature interview to Colossos magazine. In the interview, Jeff revealed that in January 2024, he had been in talks with investors, offering to only consider investment terms valuing the company at $1 billion. When an investor accepted this valuation, Jeff spent a weekend carefully considering it but ultimately decided to decline. Other team members, including Hyperliquid co-founder iliensinc (who manages the funds), found it difficult to accept, as iliensinc had already spent two weeks negotiating with investors. When Jeff called the fund, they were equally incredulous, assuming he had simply accepted terms from another institution. Jeff offered an analogy: "Hyperliquid isn't a company, it's an agreement. 'Neutralization' has been a core principle from the beginning. If Bitcoin had received venture capital funding back then, it wouldn't be the Bitcoin we know today; its entire value proposition would have been destroyed." For more details, see "Jeff Yan's Hyper Life."

data.title: Hyperliquid Founders Discuss Personal Safety Concerns
data.description:
Hyperliquid founder Jeff Yan and his team were recently featured in an exclusive interview with Colossos magazine. According to Odaily, during the interview, Jeff and co-founder iliensinc addressed issues related to personal safety.

iliensinc revealed that Jeff is the only team member who does not use a pseudonym. Initially, Hyperliquid's office was located in a shared workspace in the financial district. However, as Jeff's reputation grew, the team attracted increasing attention. Initially, this was limited to people trying to recognize Jeff's face, but it escalated to strangers approaching him and even following him into his apartment elevator. Consequently, Hyperliquid relocated to a quieter location, a building where they were less likely to be found.

Jeff also moved to a more secure residence and hired bodyguards, ensuring he is accompanied by two private security personnel during outings. iliensinc regularly trains team members on how to respond if strangers inquire about their workplace.

For more details, refer to "Jeff Yan's Hyper Life."

data.title: The founder of Trade.xyz purchased BTC at $66 per unit in 2013 and originally had no intention of starting a business.
data.description: Odaiy Planet Daily reports that Hyperliquid founder Jeff Yan and his team recently gave a feature interview to Colossos magazine. The founder of the Hyperliquid ecosystem project Trade.xyz (who requested anonymity) revealed that he purchased his first Bitcoin for $66 in 2013 and has since been active as an investor, not a builder. He originally had no intention of starting a business, and if it weren't for Jeff, he might have left the crypto industry long ago. More details can be found in "Jeff Yan's Hyper Life".

data.title: Hyperliquid founder: The biggest challenge we faced last year was server latency, which forced the team to rewrite the system from the ground up.
data.description: Odaiy Planet Daily reports that Hyperliquid founder Jeff Yan and his team recently gave a feature interview to Colossos magazine. In the interview, Jeff was asked what the biggest challenge he would face in 2025 was. He didn't mention JellyJelly's attack on JLP, nor did he mention competitors or bodyguards; instead, he mentioned API server issues. Throughout the summer, as Bitcoin broke $100,000, Hyperliquid's monthly trading volume exceeded $400 billion, and the servers connecting market makers to the blockchain began to experience problems. More and more institutions connected, each sending massive amounts of orders, cancellations, and update requests. The infrastructure transmitting this data couldn't keep up, and orders that should have been executed instantly began to be delayed by up to 3 seconds. The blockchain itself didn't crash, and user funds remained safe. But in a market where milliseconds determine victory or defeat, a 3-second delay is a serious problem. Jeff stated that he barely slept during those weeks—falling asleep at 1:30 AM and being woken up at 3 AM because the system was having problems again. Ultimately, the team rewrote the entire server system from the ground up, and the new system withstood the test of the subsequent 1011 extreme market conditions. For more details, please refer to "Jeff Yan's *Hyper Life*".

data.title: Bitcoin's Market Momentum Shows Strong Bullish Sentiment
data.description:
On April 14, glassnode released its weekly market insights, highlighting a 51.7% increase in Bitcoin's price momentum, surpassing previous highs. According to BlockBeats, this surge indicates a significant strengthening of spot buying power and market support. Meanwhile, Bitcoin futures open interest continues to rise, and options delta skew suggests a reduction in bearish bias. Combined with a cooling of on-chain activity, these factors imply a consolidation phase with improved sentiment.

glassnode noted that market pressure has eased from a profitability perspective. Overall, various indicators collectively suggest a robust bullish sentiment, increased investor participation, and enhanced risk appetite, despite signs of caution and market stabilization.

data.title: Hyperliquid founder Jeff was followed into an elevator, after which he moved his office and residence and hired bodyguards.
data.description: Odaiy Planet Daily reports that Hyperliquid founder Jeff Yan and his team recently gave a feature interview to Colossos magazine. In the interview, Jeff and co-founder iliensinc discussed issues related to personal safety. iliensinc revealed that Jeff is the only member of the team who doesn't use an alias. Hyperliquid's initial office was located in a shared workspace in the financial district, but as Jeff's fame grew, so did the outside world's attention. Initially, it was just some stares and people trying to recognize Jeff's face; then strangers started approaching him; later, someone followed him into his apartment building's elevator. Therefore, Hyperliquid moved to a quieter location, a building where no one would think to look for them. Jeff himself also moved to a safer residence, hired bodyguards, and was accompanied by two private security personnel when traveling. iliensinc also repeatedly trains team members on how to answer if strangers ask where they work. More details can be found in "Jeff Yan's Hyper Life".

data.title: Binance to Renew RLUSD Simple Earn Campaign with Up to 8% APR
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform is set to renew its RLUSD Simple Earn Flexible Products campaign, offering users the opportunity to earn up to 8% APR during the promotion period. This initiative will run from 2026-04-14 00:00:00 (UTC) to 2026-04-27 23:59:59 (UTC), allowing participants to benefit from both Real-Time APR and an exclusive Bonus Tiered APR. The subscription process is based on a first-come, first-served basis, with rewards distributed daily to users' Spot Accounts. The Real-Time APR is accrued every minute and directly accumulated in users' Earn Accounts.

The RLUSD Flexible Products offer a tiered reward system, with subscription amounts influencing the APR received. Users can subscribe to these products with a minimum limit of 0.01 RLUSD and a maximum limit of 300,000 RLUSD per user, while the total subscription limit for all users is capped at 22,000,000 RLUSD. The Bonus Tiered APR rewards begin accruing the day after subscription, based on a daily snapshot of subscribed amounts. Redemption of Flexible Products will halt the accrual of Bonus Tiered APR rewards for the redeemed amount on that day.

Participants must complete identity verification during the promotion period to qualify for rewards, and only master accounts are eligible. Binance emphasizes that the availability of products and features may vary by region, and users are responsible for understanding any restrictions related to accessing Binance services. Changes to the Simple Earn Rewards Rate will be announced on the platform, and users are encouraged to review the Binance Simple Earn Terms & Conditions and Risk Warning for more information. Binance reserves the right to amend or cancel the promotion at its discretion.

data.title: People's Daily Criticizes Misleading Crypto Promotions
data.description: People's Daily has criticized financial self-media accounts for using misleading claims like '100x coins' and promises of monthly crypto profits in the millions to attract traffic to virtual currency trading. According to NS3.AI, the publication highlighted a previous notice from the People's Bank of China and seven other departments, which states that virtual currency activities are considered illegal financial activities.

data.title: Base Ecosystem Tokens Experience Significant Daily Gains Amid Market Recovery
data.description:
On April 14, several tokens within the Base ecosystem saw notable increases in trading activity, driven by a market recovery. According to BlockBeats, multiple tokens experienced daily gains exceeding 10%. Among the tokens with higher market capitalizations:

REI reported a market capitalization of $21.1 million, with a 24-hour increase of 12%.

CLANKER's market capitalization stood at $28.3 million, showing a 24-hour rise of 9%.

BRETT had a market capitalization of $74 million, with a 24-hour growth of 8%.

ELSA's market capitalization was $69.3 million, with a 24-hour increase of 7.5%.

data.title: Y Combinator Completes First Stablecoin Investment in Prediction Market Startup
data.description:
Y Combinator has made its first investment entirely in stablecoins, according to BlockBeats. The renowned startup accelerator invested $500,000 USDC in the prediction market startup Totalis through three transactions on the Solana blockchain. The assets are being managed by the financial operations platform Ramp.

Garry Tan, CEO of Y Combinator, announced that the accelerator will offer stablecoin payment options to all YC-supported startups, not just those in the crypto sector.

Totalis plans to use Ramp to facilitate both stablecoin and fiat transactions, and intends to pay credit card bills using stablecoin accounts.
